question_structure.context: Hot isostatic pressing (HIP) equipment is used for material processing. During operation, an inert gas is first compressed into a pre-evacuated furnace chamber at room temperature. The furnace chamber is then heated to utilize the high-temperature and high-pressure environment to process the materials placed inside. In a particular instance, after a solid material was placed into the furnace chamber of a HIP device, the remaining volume was $0.13\mathsf{m}^{3}$. After evacuating the furnace chamber, 10 bottles of argon gas were compressed into the chamber at room temperature. Each argon gas bottle has a volume of $3.2{\times}10^{-2}\mathrm{m}^{3}$, and the gas pressure inside the bottle before use was $1.5{\times}10^{7}\mathsf{Pa}$, decreasing to $2.0{\times}10^{6}\mathsf{Pa}$ after use. The room temperature is $27^{\circ}C$. Argon gas can be treated as an ideal gas.
question_structure.sub_question_1: Determine the pressure of the gas inside the furnace chamber at room temperature after the argon gas has been injected.
question_structure.sub_question_2: Calculate the pressure of the gas inside the furnace chamber when it is heated to $1227^{\circ}C$ after the argon gas has been injected.

sub_question_1.step_1: Let the initial volume of each gas cylinder be $V_{0}$ and the initial pressure be $p_{0}$; the remaining pressure in the cylinder after use is $p_{1}$. Assuming that the gas with volume $V_{0}$ and pressure $p_{0}$ expands to volume $V_{1}$ when the pressure changes to $p_{1}$, from Boyle's law we have: $p_{0}V_{0}=p_{1}V_{1}$
sub_question_1.step_2: The volume of gas that is pushed into the furnace chamber at room temperature and pressure $p_{1}$ is: $V^{\prime}=V_{1}-V_{0}$
sub_question_1.step_3: Let the pressure of the gas in the furnace chamber after 10 cylinders have been compressed into it be $p_{2}$, with a volume of $V_{2}$. From Boyle's law, we have: $p_{2}V_{2}=10p_{1}V^{\prime}$
sub_question_1.step_4: Combining the equations from step_1, step_2, and step_3 and substituting the data yields: $p_{2}=3.2 \times 10^{7}\mathrm{Pa}$

sub_question_2.step_5: Let the temperature of the furnace chamber before heating be $T_{0}$, the temperature of the furnace chamber after heating be $T_{1}$, and the gas pressure be $p_{3}$. From Gay-Lussac's law, we have: ${\frac{p_{3}}{T_{1}}}={\frac{p_{2}}{T_{0}}}$
sub_question_2.step_6: Combining the results from step_4 and step_5 and substituting the data yields: $p_{3}=1.6 \times 10^{8}\mathrm{{Pa}}$

result_quantity.name: Volume of gas pushed into the furnace chamber at room temperature and pressure $p_{1}$

step_4.physical_theorem: Solving simultaneous equations and data substitution

result_quantity.name: Pressure of the gas in the furnace chamber after 10 cylinders have been compressed into it
Pressure of the gas inside the furnace chamber when it is heated to $1227^{\circ}C$
